terms.definition: L2 distance d₂(u,v) = sqrt(Σ(uᵢ−vᵢ)²); Minkowski with p=2; default geometry for k-means and RBF kernels.
L1 distance d₁(u,v) = Σ|uᵢ−vᵢ|; Minkowski with p=1; sums axis-aligned gaps between points.
Lp family d_p(u,v) = (Σ|uᵢ−vᵢ|^p)^(1/p) for p≥1; generalizes Manhattan, Euclidean and approaches max-gap as p→∞.
Dot product divided by the product of L2 norms; measures direction alignment, standard for embedding retrieval when magnitude is uninformative.
In ML practice, a function ranking how far apart two feature vectors are; choice defines k-NN neighbors and influences clustering and retrieval behavior.
